text: What is a nutmeg in football?
options: Playing the ball through an opponent's legs
Scoring from a corner kick
A goalkeeper punching the ball
A pass made with the heel only
correct_answer: Playing the ball through an opponent's legs

text: What is a bicycle kick in football?
options: An acrobatic overhead kick
A pass made while cycling possession
A kick taken from the center circle
A goalkeeper clearance from hand
correct_answer: An acrobatic overhead kick

text: What is a back four in football tactics?
options: A defensive line of four players
Four forwards behind the striker
Four goalkeepers on the bench
Four passes before every shot
correct_answer: A defensive line of four players

text: What is man-marking in football?
options: Assigning a defender to track a specific opponent
Marking the halfway line with paint
A goalkeeper choosing a penalty side
A referee writing down goals
correct_answer: Assigning a defender to track a specific opponent

text: What is zonal marking in football?
options: Defending assigned spaces rather than specific players
Marking players only by shirt number
A rule used only for penalty kicks
A way to decide substitutions
correct_answer: Defending assigned spaces rather than specific players

text: What is a counterattack in football?
options: A quick attack launched after winning possession
A pass back to restart the match
A defender counting opponents
A shot after the final whistle
correct_answer: A quick attack launched after winning possession

text: What is an indirect free kick in football?
options: A free kick that must touch another player before a goal can count
A free kick taken only by the goalkeeper
A kick awarded after every offside goal
A kick that must go backward
correct_answer: A free kick that must touch another player before a goal can count

text: What does it mean for the ball to be out of play in football?
options: The whole ball fully crosses a boundary line or play is stopped
Any part of the ball touches a line
A player lets the ball bounce
The goalkeeper catches the ball
correct_answer: The whole ball fully crosses a boundary line or play is stopped

text: What is a set piece in football?
options: A planned restart such as a corner or free kick
A fixed player salary
A shirt number reserved for captains
A training cone only
correct_answer: A planned restart such as a corner or free kick

text: What is the technical area in football?
options: The marked area where coaches and substitutes stay near the bench
The penalty spot
The center circle only
The goalkeeper's six-yard box
correct_answer: The marked area where coaches and substitutes stay near the bench

text: What is an alley-oop in basketball?
options: A pass caught near the basket and scored in one motion
A bounce pass from the referee
A free throw after halftime
A turnover at center court
correct_answer: A pass caught near the basket and scored in one motion

text: What is a crossover dribble?
options: A dribble switching the ball quickly from one hand to the other
A pass thrown across the crowd
A shot crossing the backboard
A referee crossing arms to stop play
correct_answer: A dribble switching the ball quickly from one hand to the other

text: What is a full-court press?
options: Defensive pressure applied across most or all of the court
A shot from one baseline to the other
A halftime media interview
A team photo before the game
correct_answer: Defensive pressure applied across most or all of the court

text: What is a turnover in basketball?
options: Losing possession to the other team
Changing jerseys at halftime
Making two layups in a row
Winning the opening tip
correct_answer: Losing possession to the other team

text: What is a double-dribble violation?
options: Dribbling again after stopping a dribble
Dribbling with both teams at once
Scoring two baskets in one shot
Taking two free throws
correct_answer: Dribbling again after stopping a dribble

text: What is a post-up in basketball?
options: An offensive move using position near the basket with back to defender
A shot from behind half court
A timeout after a rebound
A defensive signal by the scorer
correct_answer: An offensive move using position near the basket with back to defender

text: What is transition offense?
options: Offense moving quickly after a change of possession
Offense used only after a timeout
Shooting only free throws
Playing with four defenders
correct_answer: Offense moving quickly after a change of possession

text: What is the key in basketball?
options: The painted lane area near the basket
The locker room entrance
The referee's whistle cord
The final possession only
correct_answer: The painted lane area near the basket

text: What does it mean to foul out in basketball?
options: A player is disqualified after reaching the foul limit
A player misses all free throws
A coach leaves the bench
A team loses the jump ball
correct_answer: A player is disqualified after reaching the foul limit

text: What is an inbound pass?
options: A pass from out of bounds to restart play
A pass into the locker room
A pass only after a dunk
A pass from the crowd to a player
correct_answer: A pass from out of bounds to restart play

text: What is a rally in tennis?
options: A sequence of shots after the serve before the point ends
A player speech before the match
A ball change ceremony
A coin toss only
correct_answer: A sequence of shots after the serve before the point ends

text: What is a passing shot in tennis?
options: A shot hit past an opponent at the net
A serve that touches the umpire chair
A shot hit after the match ends
A ball handed to a ball kid
correct_answer: A shot hit past an opponent at the net

text: What is an unforced error in tennis?
options: A mistake not caused by strong pressure from the opponent
A referee's scoring error
A serve that is always replayed
A shot that wins the point instantly
correct_answer: A mistake not caused by strong pressure from the opponent

text: What is a winner in tennis?
options: A shot the opponent cannot reach or return legally
A player who wins the toss only
A serve that clips the net and lands in
A practice shot before warm-up
correct_answer: A shot the opponent cannot reach or return legally

text: What is a service game in tennis?
options: A game in which a player is serving
A game played without rackets
A game after the match is over
A game where only volleys count
correct_answer: A game in which a player is serving

text: What is a set point in tennis?
options: A point that can win the set
The first point of a match
A point after a ball change only
A replayed point after every let
correct_answer: A point that can win the set

text: What is a match point in tennis?
options: A point that can win the match
A point that starts a tiebreak
The point after love
A practice serve before play begins
correct_answer: A point that can win the match

text: What is topspin in tennis?
options: Forward spin that makes the ball dip and bounce higher
A shot with no rotation
A serve hit below the net
A racket string pattern only
correct_answer: Forward spin that makes the ball dip and bounce higher

text: What is the net player usually trying to do in doubles tennis?
options: Cut off volleys and pressure opponents
Stand behind the back fence
Serve every point
Avoid touching the ball
correct_answer: Cut off volleys and pressure opponents

text: What is a hold of serve in tennis?
options: The server wins their service game
The receiver wins the game
A player grips the racket too long
A ball kid delays play
correct_answer: The server wins their service game

text: What is a cockpit in motorsport?
options: The driver's seating and control area
The tire storage room
The podium stage
The timing tower
correct_answer: The driver's seating and control area

text: What is a marshal in motorsport?
options: A track official who helps with safety and signaling
A driver who starts last
A sponsor representative only
A tire compound
correct_answer: A track official who helps with safety and signaling

text: What is a red flag in racing?
options: A signal that the session is stopped
A signal to speed up
A signal for final lap only
A signal for fastest lap award
correct_answer: A signal that the session is stopped

text: What is the pit wall in motorsport?
options: The area where team strategists and engineers monitor the race
The wall behind a trophy cabinet
A tire barrier in every corner
A driver's hotel area
correct_answer: The area where team strategists and engineers monitor the race

text: What is a flying lap?
options: A timed lap begun at speed rather than from a standing start
A lap completed in an aircraft
A lap behind a tow truck
A lap after the race has ended
correct_answer: A timed lap begun at speed rather than from a standing start

text: What is a standing start?
options: A race start from stationary grid positions
A start while drivers stand outside cars
A restart from pit lane only
A start with engines switched off
correct_answer: A race start from stationary grid positions

text: What is a rolling start?
options: A start where cars are moving before racing begins
A start with cars on trailers
A start after tire removal
A start from the garage only
correct_answer: A start where cars are moving before racing begins

text: What is telemetry in motorsport?
options: Data sent from the vehicle to engineers
A driver's autograph session
A type of racing fuel only
A tire storage shelf
correct_answer: Data sent from the vehicle to engineers

text: What is a sector on a racing circuit?
options: A timed section of the lap
A team garage number only
A driver's salary bracket
A type of racing helmet
correct_answer: A timed section of the lap

text: What is a lap record?
options: The fastest officially recorded lap for a track or session type
The number of laps left on fuel
A driver's music playlist
The final starting grid
correct_answer: The fastest officially recorded lap for a track or session type

text: What is a cutman in combat sports?
options: A cornerman who treats cuts and swelling between rounds
A judge who cuts scores in half
A fighter who only throws elbows
A referee who stops all grappling
correct_answer: A cornerman who treats cuts and swelling between rounds

text: What is a corner in boxing or MMA?
options: A fighter's team area and support staff
A judge's private room
A banned strike
The center of the ring
correct_answer: A fighter's team area and support staff

text: What is a feint in combat sports?
options: A deceptive movement to draw a reaction
A confirmed knockout
A post-fight interview
A type of hand wrap
correct_answer: A deceptive movement to draw a reaction

text: What is a jab in boxing?
options: A quick straight punch with the lead hand
A spinning kick
A choke from the back
A wrestling throw
correct_answer: A quick straight punch with the lead hand

text: What is a cross in boxing?
options: A straight punch usually thrown with the rear hand
A kick to the leg
A referee's warning gesture
A grappling pin
correct_answer: A straight punch usually thrown with the rear hand

text: What is a hook in boxing?
options: A punch thrown in a curved motion from the side
A high jump kick
A towel thrown after a win
A wrestling escape only
correct_answer: A punch thrown in a curved motion from the side

text: What is an uppercut in boxing?
options: An upward punch aimed from below
A kick to the shoulder
A takedown at the legs
A judge's highest score
correct_answer: An upward punch aimed from below

text: What is a leg kick in combat sports?
options: A kick aimed at an opponent's leg
A referee's walking signal
A boxing-only punch
A wrestling pin escape
correct_answer: A kick aimed at an opponent's leg

text: What is an armbar?
options: A joint lock attacking the elbow
A scoring table
A boxing ring rope
A fighter's entrance song
correct_answer: A joint lock attacking the elbow

text: What does TKO stand for in combat sports?
options: Technical Knockout
Timed Kick Off
Total Knock Over
Tournament Kick Order
correct_answer: Technical Knockout

text: What is a headshot in many shooter games?
options: A hit to an opponent's head area
A player's profile photo
A tournament poster
A camera view of a coach
correct_answer: A hit to an opponent's head area

text: What is recoil in shooter games?
options: Weapon kickback after firing
A player leaving a team
A map loading screen
A prize ceremony
correct_answer: Weapon kickback after firing

text: What is aim training?
options: Practice focused on improving targeting accuracy
A coach picking team uniforms
A spectator voting system
A network setup guide
correct_answer: Practice focused on improving targeting accuracy

text: What is map control in competitive games?
options: Holding important areas to gain strategic advantage
Changing the game's graphics only
Choosing random spawn points
Deleting the map file
correct_answer: Holding important areas to gain strategic advantage

text: What is a rotation in team-based esports?
options: Moving players between map areas or objectives
Turning the monitor sideways
Changing the team logo weekly
Replacing every game rule
correct_answer: Moving players between map areas or objectives

text: What is a reset in competitive games?
options: Returning to a neutral state or starting a new round/attempt
Deleting a player's account only
Turning off all sound
A trophy ceremony
correct_answer: Returning to a neutral state or starting a new round/attempt

text: What does AFK mean in gaming?
options: Away From Keyboard
Attack First Kill
Arena Final Knockout
Aim Fast Keybind
correct_answer: Away From Keyboard

text: What is a ban phase in many competitive games?
options: A stage where teams remove certain characters, maps, or options
A period where all players are suspended
A break for hardware cleaning
A stage where prize money is counted
correct_answer: A stage where teams remove certain characters, maps, or options

text: What is a pick phase in many competitive games?
options: A stage where teams choose characters, maps, or options
A stage where referees choose fans
A stage where every player disconnects
A stage only for changing keyboards
correct_answer: A stage where teams choose characters, maps, or options

text: What is a spectator mode?
options: A mode for watching a match without playing
A mode that removes all viewers
A mode only for changing usernames
A mode that disables the scoreboard
correct_answer: A mode for watching a match without playing
